executive_summary: A role-based inventory and order management system for internal store refills and third-party bulk supply. Store managers request products on mobile, warehouse packs and dispatches, stores confirm receipt. All stock movements tracked with full audit trail. No pricing, no payroll in MVP. Built for speed, reliability, and offline resilience.

platforms.app_model: Single app experience with role-based login and role-specific UI
platforms.backend: Shared REST API with role-based access control

business_model.primary_use_case_1: Internal store inventory refill (stores request, warehouse fulfills)
business_model.primary_use_case_2: Bulk supply to third-party stores (admin creates, warehouse fulfills)

goals: Provide fast, minimal product listing and inventory system
Enable store managers to request inventory refills via app
Prevent overselling with reserved stock model
Track every stock movement with timestamp, actor, and reason
Ensure warehouse dispatch + store confirmation two-person approval
Support bulk orders for third-party clients without pricing
Maintain offline-first mobile experience with sync-on-reconnect
Audit all critical actions for compliance and debugging
Role-based visibility (stores see only their own orders)

operational: Order completion in < 4 hours (request → dispatch → receive)
Mobile app works offline, syncs when connection restored

superadmin.description: Watches everything. Creates accounts. Manages roles. Does NOT touch operations. Sole job: see the full picture + manage who has access.

visibility.data_scope: All stores, all warehouses, all users, all orders
visibility.dashboard: Global KPIs across all warehouses and stores simultaneously, live order pipeline, inventory health, staff attendance, recent activity feed, low stock alerts, revenue/volume charts, orders over time, top products, warehouse utilization, store restock frequency

dashboard: Global analytics dashboard with all 6 charts
Live order pipeline — how many at each stage right now
Staff attendance summary across all locations today
Recent activity feed — every action by every user
Low stock alerts across all locations
Orders over time — all locations, all types
user_and_account_management: Create user accounts for any role (superadmin, warehouse manager, store manager, staff)
Edit user details — name, email, phone
Assign or reassign user to a location
Activate, deactivate, or block any account
Create staff IDs (employee codes) — EMP-001 format
View all staff across all locations
role_management: View all roles and their permissions
Reassign a warehouse manager to a different warehouse
Reassign a store manager to a different store
locations: Set geofence coordinates and radius per location
third_party_clients: Create and manage bulk order clients
analytics_read_only: All 6 charts — orders, inventory, products, stock flow, status mix, store comparison
audit_log: Full history of every action by every user
Filter by role, user, action, date
products: Edit product (title, brand, category, model, color, status, custom_style)
Delete product (soft delete to preserve history)
bulk_orders: Create bulk order for any third-party client

warehouse_manager.description: Owns the warehouse completely. Approves incoming store order requests. Fulfills them. Manages their people.

visibility.data_scope: Only their assigned warehouse(s) + pending orders for their warehouse
visibility.dashboard: Pack queue, dispatch queue, pending approvals, low stock in warehouse, today's staff attendance, dispatched today count, KPIs

dashboard: Pack queue — confirmed orders ready to pick
Dispatch queue — packed orders ready to ship
Pending approvals — store order requests waiting
KPIs: to approve, to pack, to dispatch, low stock
store_order_approval: View all pending store order requests from all stores (assigned to their warehouse)
View order details — which store, which products, quantities
Approve order — reserves stock, notifies store manager
Reject order — with required reason, notifies store manager
View all orders by status for their warehouse
order_fulfillment: Mark order as packed — with item checklist
Mark order as dispatched — with optional notes
bulk_orders: View bulk orders assigned to their warehouse
inventory_warehouse: View all products and stock levels
products: View full product catalogue (all products, search + filter)
Create new product — with image upload
Set initial stock at their warehouse on creation
staff_management: Create staff account for new staff member (their warehouse)
Set designation, joining date, working days
View all staff at their warehouse
Today's attendance overview — who is present, late, absent
View any staff member's attendance history
Create and assign tasks to their staff
Set task priority and due date
reports: View order fulfillment report (pending orders, packed, dispatched)

cannot_do: See store orders they did not fulfill

store_manager.description: Owns the store completely. Creates restock requests. Confirms what arrives. Manages their people.

visibility.data_scope: Only their store's data, their store's orders, their store's inventory
visibility.dashboard: Action required banner, pending orders, arriving soon, inventory health, staff attendance, recent orders, low stock, quick restock CTA

dashboard: Action required banner when dispatched orders need confirmation
Low stock items for their store
store_orders: Create order request — select products from full catalogue, set quantities
See warehouse available stock before requesting
View all their store's orders (own store only)
Confirm receipt of dispatched orders — full item checklist
inventory_store: View their store's inventory only (available, reserved, total)
staff_management: Create staff account for new staff member (their store)
Set designation, joining date, working days
View all staff at their store
View any staff member's attendance history
Create and assign tasks to their staff
Set task priority and due date

staff.description: Operational staff at warehouse or store. Check-in/check-out, complete assigned tasks.

visibility.data_scope: Self only — their attendance, assigned tasks
visibility.dashboard: Today's tasks, check-in status, attendance summary

products: Edit product (title, brand, category, model, color, status, custom_style)
Delete product (soft delete to preserve history)
store_orders: Approve or reject store order requests
bulk_orders: Create bulk order for any third-party client
locations: Edit location (name, address, geofence coordinates, radius)
third_party_clients: Edit client (name, contact, GST, credit limit)

cannot_do: Bypass state machine (e.g., mark order complete without store confirmation)

warehouse_manager.description: Manages warehouse inventory, dispatch of store refill orders and bulk orders, and stock movement tracking.

visibility.data_scope: Only their assigned warehouse(s) + pending orders for their warehouse
visibility.dashboard: Warehouse-specific dashboard (pending orders, low stock, dispatch queue)

products: View product availability in their warehouse
inventory: View stock movements for their warehouse
Manual stock adjustment (with reason, logged, approval required from superadmin)
store_orders: View store orders assigned to their warehouse (pending + history)
bulk_orders: View bulk orders assigned to their warehouse (pending + history)
Cannot create or approve bulk orders
stock_transfer: View transfer requests for their warehouse
reports: View order fulfillment report (pending orders, packed, dispatched)
Cannot access client or user reports

cannot_do: Change order status backward (no cancel after dispatch)

store_manager.description: Manages their assigned store's inventory and initiates refill orders. Can request products from warehouse and confirm receipt.

visibility.data_scope: Only their store's data, their store's orders, their store's inventory
visibility.dashboard: Store-specific dashboard (their inventory, pending orders, order history)

products: View product availability in their store
inventory: View their store's inventory (available, reserved, total)
View stock movements for their store
store_orders: View their store's orders only (pending + history)
Create new order request (select products, quantities)
Mark order as 'store_received' (confirm all items arrived)
bulk_orders: No access (bulk orders are third-party, not for stores)

ui_sections: Create Order Request (request products from warehouse)
cannot_do: See other stores' data or orders

authentication_and_access.description: Role-based login with permission-controlled UI and API access, plus audit trail.
features: Location assigned to user (store manager → store, warehouse manager → warehouse)
Permission-based API access (backend enforces, frontend respects)
Audit log: all login attempts, successful and failed
production_requirements: Rate limiting: max 5 failed login attempts per IP per minute
Session timeout: auto-logout after 24 hours or 30 minutes of inactivity
All login events logged (timestamp, IP, device, success/failure)

product_listing.description: Minimal, fast product listing focused on scannability and quick actions.

status_rules.inactive: Hidden from store order UI, warehouse can still see
status_rules.discontinued: Not available for any order, visible in history only

ui_layout: Quick action buttons (View, Request [stores], Add to Bulk [admin])

list_behaviors.search: By title, brand, model, short_name, sku
list_behaviors.pagination: 50 items per page, lazy load on scroll

production_requirements: Image CDN (resize to 300x300 for mobile list)
Search response < 500ms (indexed on title, brand, model)

inventory_management.description: Track stock by location with reserved stock to prevent overselling.
rules: Store stock can only increase via warehouse dispatch
Reserved stock cannot be sold or transferred again
All stock changes logged with timestamp, actor, reason
features: View inventory per product per location
View available vs reserved vs total
Manual stock adjustment (superadmin with reason + approval log)
Low stock alert (configurable threshold per product)
Stock movement history (queryable by date, actor, location, product)
Inventory sync across app and web
production_requirements: Row-level locks during stock operations (prevent race conditions)
Cache inventory locally on mobile (TTL: 5 minutes)

store_order_request.description: Store managers request inventory refill from warehouse on mobile app.
workflow: Store Manager: Opens 'Create Order Request'
Store Manager: Searches and selects products (e.g., 5x Samsung TV)
Store Manager: System validates: available_stock ≥ requested qty
Store Manager: Submits request (status: 'draft')
System: Superadmin can review pending requests
Superadmin: Approves request (status: 'confirmed', creates reservation)
System: Reserved stock locked (warehouse available ↓)
Warehouse Manager: Sees pending order in 'Dispatch Queue'
Warehouse Manager: Packs items, marks 'packed' (status: 'packed')
Warehouse Manager: Ships items, marks 'dispatched' (status: 'dispatched')
Warehouse Manager: Generates shipping label (order_id)
Store Manager: Receives notification 'Order dispatched, confirmation needed'
Store Manager: Opens app, sees order, inspects physical items
Store Manager: Confirms 'All items received' (status: 'store_received')
System: Updates inventory (warehouse stock ↓, store stock ↑, reserved ↓)
System: Order marked 'completed', audit trail closed

statuses.completed: Order finalized, all stock movements done

fields: approved_by (superadmin user_id, nullable if not approved)

permissions.store_manager: Create (their own store only), View own orders, Mark as received
permissions.superadmin: View all, Approve/Reject, Cancel, View audit trail
permissions.warehouse_manager: View assigned warehouse orders, Mark as packed/dispatched

production_requirements: Idempotency key on creation (prevent duplicate orders on mobile retry)
State machine enforcement (only valid transitions allowed)
Notification: Store alerted when dispatched (push + in-app)
Timeout alert: If store doesn't confirm within 24 hours, escalate to superadmin

bulk_order_management.description: Superadmin creates orders for third-party client stores. No pricing, no payment tracking.
workflow: Superadmin: Selects third-party client store from list
Superadmin: Searches products, selects qty (e.g., 10x Samsung TV, 5x LG Fridge)
System: Validates warehouse has available_stock ≥ requested qty
Superadmin: Submits bulk order (status: 'draft')
System: No approval needed (superadmin creates = approved)
System: Status changed to 'confirmed', stock reserved
Warehouse Manager: Sees order in 'Bulk Order Queue'
Warehouse Manager: Packs items, marks 'packed'
Warehouse Manager: Ships items, marks 'dispatched'
System: No store confirmation needed (client handles externally)
System: Inventory updated, order marked 'completed'

permissions.superadmin: Create, View all, Cancel, View audit trail
permissions.warehouse_manager: View assigned orders, Mark as packed/dispatched

production_requirements: No pricing fields (kept simple for MVP)

third_party_client_management.description: Client records for third-party stores receiving bulk supply.

permissions.superadmin: Create, Edit, View all, Change status

production_requirements: Status change logs (who changed it, when)

stock_transfer.description: Movement of items between warehouse and stores (MVP: warehouse-initiated transfers only).
stock_transfer.note: Store-to-warehouse and store-to-store transfers can be added in v2.1.
workflow: Warehouse Manager: Initiates transfer (from warehouse to specific store)
Warehouse Manager: Selects products and quantities
System: Validates warehouse available_stock ≥ qty
Warehouse Manager: Creates transfer (status: 'pending')
Store Manager: Receives notification 'Transfer in transit'
Warehouse Manager: Ships items, marks 'in_transit'
Store Manager: Receives items, marks 'completed'
System: Updates inventory (warehouse ↓, store ↑)

permissions.warehouse_manager: Create transfers from their warehouse, mark as in_transit
permissions.store_manager: View transfers to their store, mark as completed
permissions.superadmin: View all, cancel transfers, view audit

widgets: Orders: Pending store orders, pending bulk orders, today's dispatches
Inventory: Low stock alert, product count, warehouse stock snapshot
Operations: Users online, locations active, recent actions (audit log)
Pending Orders: Store refill orders, bulk orders (both in 'confirmed' or 'packed' status)
Inventory: Low stock items for their warehouse
Dispatch Queue: Orders ready to ship
Performance: Orders packed today, dispatch time trend
My Inventory: Total stock, available vs reserved breakdown
My Orders: Pending requests, in-progress orders, completed orders
Alerts: Stock running low, orders needing confirmation

production_requirements: Dashboard data cached (update every 30 seconds, configurable)

examples: ORD-ST01-20260412-0001 (Store order from location ST01 on April 12, 2026, first order)
BULK-WH01-20260412-0007 (Bulk order from warehouse WH01 on April 12, 2026, seventh bulk order)
rules: Location code assigned during location setup (ST01 for store 1, WH01 for warehouse 1)
Incremental number reset daily (or use hourly+minute for high volume)
implementation: Database trigger or application-level generation
Verify uniqueness before assigning
Index on order_id for fast lookup

notifications.description: Async notifications for critical events, resilient with retry logic.

store_order_confirmed.message: Store order ORD-ST01-20260412-0001 ready to pack (5x Samsung TV)

order_dispatched.message: Your order ORD-ST01-20260412-0001 is on the way. Please confirm receipt.

order_unconfirmed_24h.trigger: Order in 'dispatched' for 24+ hours, no confirmation
order_unconfirmed_24h.message: Order ORD-ST01-20260412-0001 awaiting store confirmation for 24 hours

low_stock_alert.message: Samsung 55-inch TV stock low (2 units remaining)

bulk_order_created.message: Bulk order BULK-WH01-20260412-0007 for ABC Retail Store (10x TV, 5x Fridge)

production_requirements: Use job queue (e.g., Bull on Redis or database-backed queue)
Retry failed notifications: 3 attempts with exponential backoff (5s, 30s, 2m)
Email rate limit: max 1 email per user per event per hour

constraints: superadmin must not have location_id (or location_id = null)

permissions.view: superadmin (all), warehouse_manager (their warehouse users only), store_manager (cannot view other users)
superadmin (all), warehouse_manager (their locations only), store_manager (their location only)

permissions.update: only via stock movement events (never direct update)

fields: from_location_id (foreign key to locations, nullable if external in)
stock_movements.immutability: Movements are immutable. Never updated or deleted. Always appended.

permissions.view: superadmin (all), warehouse_manager (their warehouse movements), store_manager (their store movements)

fields: store_id (foreign key to locations where type='store')
warehouse_id (foreign key to locations where type='warehouse')
status (draft | confirmed | packed | dispatched | store_received | completed | cancelled)

permissions.view: superadmin (all), warehouse_manager (their warehouse), store_manager (their store only)

fields: warehouse_id (foreign key to locations where type='warehouse')
status (draft | confirmed | packed | dispatched | completed | cancelled)
order_id (foreign key to store_orders or bulk_orders)
action (create | update | delete | approve | dispatch | confirm_receive | cancel)
audit_logs.immutability: Audit logs are immutable, never updated or deleted.

store_order_complete_flow: 1. Store Manager: Opens app, navigates to 'Create Order Request'
2. Store Manager: Searches products (e.g., 'Samsung TV')
3. Store Manager: Selects product, enters qty (system validates: available_stock ≥ qty)
4. Store Manager: Adds more items if needed
5. Store Manager: Reviews order summary, confirms submission
6. System: Creates order with status='draft', generates order_id (e.g., ORD-ST01-20260412-0001)
7. System: Audit log created (store_manager created order)
8. Warehouse Manager: Receives notification 'New order request from Store 01'
9. Warehouse Manager: Reviews order in dashboard
10. Warehouse Manager: Clicks 'Approve', confirms (checks stock availability)
11. System: Status → 'confirmed', stock reserved (available_stock ↓, reserved_stock ↑)
12. System: Creates stock_movement (type='order_reserved')
13. System: Warehouse Manager notified 'Order ORD-ST01-20260412-0001 ready to pack' (same user)
14. Warehouse Manager: Opens app, sees order in 'Dispatch Queue'
15. Warehouse Manager: Scans product barcodes, confirms items match
16. Warehouse Manager: Clicks 'Mark as Packed', enters dispatch notes (optional)
17. System: Status → 'packed', audit log created
18. Warehouse Manager: Prints shipping label (order_id barcode)
19. Warehouse Manager: Ships items, clicks 'Mark as Dispatched'
20. System: Status → 'dispatched', timestamp recorded
21. System: Creates stock_movement (type='order_deducted')
22. System: Store Manager receives push notification 'Order dispatched, confirmation needed'
23. Store Manager: Opens app, sees order in 'My Orders' with status='dispatched'
24. Store Manager: Physically receives items from delivery
25. Store Manager: Opens app, clicks order, verifies items match (qty + product)
26. Store Manager: Clicks 'Confirm Receipt', confirms
27. System: Status → 'store_received', timestamp recorded
28. System: Creates stock_movement (type='order_issued', warehouse ↓, store ↑)
29. System: Updates inventory (warehouse: available↓, store: available↑)
30. System: Status → 'completed', order finalized
31. System: Audit log closed, all movements logged
bulk_order_complete_flow: 1. Superadmin: Opens web app, navigates to 'Create Bulk Order'
2. Superadmin: Selects third-party client store (e.g., 'ABC Retail Store')
3. Superadmin: Searches products, adds items and quantities (e.g., 10x Samsung TV, 5x LG Fridge)
4. System: Validates warehouse available_stock ≥ qty for each item
5. Superadmin: Reviews order summary, confirms submission
6. System: Creates order with status='draft', generates order_id (e.g., 'BULK-WH01-20260412-0007')
8. System: Warehouse Manager notified 'Bulk order BULK-WH01-20260412-0007 for ABC Retail Store'
9. Warehouse Manager: Views order in 'Bulk Order Queue'
10. Warehouse Manager: Picks and packs items
13. Warehouse Manager: Ships items, clicks 'Mark as Dispatched'
16. No store confirmation needed (third-party handles externally)
transfer_request_flow: 2. Warehouse Manager: Selects product, quantity, destination store
4. Warehouse Manager: Submits, system creates transfer_id
6. Store Manager (receiving): Notified 'Transfer in transit to your store'
7. Warehouse Manager: Packs items, clicks 'Mark as In Transit'
10. Store Manager: Receives items, clicks 'Confirm Complete'

business_rules: Reserved stock created on order confirmation, locked until dispatch
Reserved stock cannot be used in another order
Discontinued products cannot be ordered (backend validation)
Inactive products hidden from store order UI
Every stock movement creates immutable audit entry
Every order must have unique, printable order_id
Warehouse stock is master source (stores receive from warehouse only)
Store managers can only see their own store's data and orders
Warehouse managers can only see their assigned warehouse(s)
Superadmin cannot be assigned to a location (superadmin = global access)
Store orders require superadmin approval before warehouse packs
Bulk orders auto-confirmed by superadmin on creation
No pricing in MVP (removed from spec)
No payroll in MVP (removed from spec)
All critical actions logged: create, approve, dispatch, receive, cancel
State machine enforcement: only valid status transitions allowed
Idempotency enforced on order creation (prevent duplicate orders on mobile retry)

idempotency_and_safety.description: Prevent duplicate state changes from network retries or duplicate requests
implementation: Every API request gets unique idempotency_key (uuid)
Client generates idempotency_key on mobile (or server generates if web)
Server stores (idempotency_key, response) in cache (Redis, 24-hour TTL)
If same idempotency_key received again, return cached response
Never execute action twice
Use database row-level locks (PostgreSQL FOR UPDATE)
On order approval: SELECT inventory FOR UPDATE, validate available_stock, update reserved_stock
On order dispatch: SELECT inventory FOR UPDATE, validate reserved_stock, deduct total_stock
Transaction timeout: 30 seconds max
Log timeout errors for debugging

audit_trail.description: Complete history of all state changes for compliance

enforcement: Validate on backend before state change

offline_resilience.description: Mobile app works offline, syncs when reconnected
implementation: Cache inventory locally on mobile (SQLite, 5-minute TTL)
Queue store order requests locally if offline
Queue order confirmations locally if offline
On reconnect: sync queued actions with conflict detection
Conflict resolution: warehouse dispatch timestamp wins (source of truth)

error_handling.description: Graceful failures with clear error messages

http_status_codes.404: Not found (order, product, location doesn't exist)

implementation: Use job queue with retry logic
Retry failed notifications 3 times (5s, 30s, 2m backoff)
Log all notification attempts
In-app notifications persistent until read

authentication: Rate limit: 5 failed login attempts per IP per minute
authorization: Cannot access data outside assigned location
data_protection: No sensitive data in API logs

focus: Status transition validation (only valid transitions allowed)
Full store order workflow: create → approve → pack → dispatch → receive → complete
Full bulk order workflow: create → pack → dispatch → complete
Concurrent orders on same product (ensure no overselling)
Stock movement logging on every state change

scenarios: Mobile app sync from 50 devices simultaneously

focus: Store manager cannot see other store's orders (data isolation)

pre_deployment: ✓ Notification queue set up (Redis or database-backed)
✓ Environment variables configured (JWT secret, API keys, etc.)
deployment: ✓ Smoke test critical workflows (create order → dispatch → receive)
post_deployment: ✓ Monitor API response times (should be < 2s p95)
✓ Verify role-based access working (test each role)
rollback_plan: If critical bug: revert to previous version within 15 minutes
Restore from database backup if data corruption
Document what went wrong for post-mortem

include: Store order request + approval + dispatch + receive flow

future_scope: Advanced pricing tiers (if needed for bulk orders)
